Bison Grammar Skeleton.description: Complete modern C++ Bison grammar: %require 3.8, api.value.type variant, api.token.constructor, %locations, %code requires / %code blocks, sample tokens, one rule, and the error() implementation. Start here for any new C++ parser.

Rule Definition.description: Single grammar rule with one production and a semantic action. The rule name goes before the colon; list symbols in the production body; write the C/C++ action inside braces. Terminate with a semicolon.

Rule Alternative.description: Additional production alternative for an existing rule. Paste after an existing production line. Each alternative shares the rule's %type.

Token Declaration.description: %token declaration with an optional C/C++ type and a string alias. The type is used with api.value.type variant; the alias enables writing "token_text" in rules instead of TOKEN_NAME.

Type Declaration.description: %type declaration associates a C/C++ type with one or more non-terminals. Required for every non-terminal when using api.value.type variant.

Code Requires Block.description: %code requires is emitted before the YYSTYPE/YYLTYPE definitions, making its content visible to both the parser header and implementation. Use it for AST node types, forward declarations, and headers that token/rule types depend on.

Code Block.description: %code (no qualifier) is emitted in the parser implementation file after the generated definitions. Use it for #includes that only the parser .cc needs, such as the scanner header or driver header.

Define Directive.description: %define sets a Bison named variable. Pick from the dropdown for common options: api.value.type controls the semantic value union/variant; parse.error verbose/detailed controls error message quality; api.token.constructor enables make_TOKEN() factories.

Precedence Left.description: Declares tokens as left-associative and sets their precedence level. Later %left/%right/%nonassoc lines have higher precedence. Used to resolve shift/reduce conflicts in expression grammars.

Precedence Right.description: Declares tokens as right-associative and sets their precedence level. Use for right-associative operators such as assignment (=) or exponentiation (**). Later declarations have higher precedence.

Precedence Nonassoc.description: Declares tokens as non-associative: using them twice in a row at the same level is a syntax error. Use for comparison operators (<, >, ==) that should not chain without parentheses.

Prologue Block.description: Legacy prologue block: code placed verbatim at the top of the generated .c/.cc file. Prefer %code or %code requires in modern grammars for better scoping control.

Empty Alternative.description: Explicit empty production (Bison 3+). Using %empty makes the intentional epsilon production visible to readers and tools, and suppresses the -Wempty-rule warning.

Prec Override.description: %prec overrides the precedence of a rule with that of a named token. Classic use-case: unary minus via %prec UMINUS to give it higher precedence than binary minus.

GLR Grammar Skeleton.description: Complete Bison GLR grammar: %glr-parser, %define parse.lac full for complete lookahead correction, and an intentionally ambiguous expression rule to illustrate GLR resolution. Use when your language is genuinely ambiguous or hard to refactor into LALR(1).

Error Recovery Rule.description: Grammar rule with panic-mode error recovery using the built-in error token. When a syntax error occurs inside a statement, Bison discards tokens until it finds the synchronisation character (default ';'), then calls yyerrok to reset the error state.

Union Declaration.description: %union declares the fields of the YYSTYPE semantic-value union for C parsers. Add one field per token/rule type. Prefer api.value.type variant for C++ parsers — it is type-safe and supports non-trivial types.

Location Tracking Skeleton.description: Bison grammar with %locations enabled. Shows @$ and @1 for location propagation in rule actions, the location_type fields (begin/end with line/column), and an error() function that prints file:line:column. Use when you need source positions in AST nodes or diagnostics.

Variant Type Skeleton.description: C++ Bison grammar focused on the api.value.type variant pattern: multiple typed tokens (int, double, string), token constructors via api.token.constructor, unary minus with %prec UMINUS, and a complete expression grammar. Contrast with bison-skeleton (which also adds %locations) — use this when you want to study or teach the variant type system specifically.

Reentrant Parser Skeleton.description: Bison parser with %param to inject a driver/context object into the parser and the error() function. Enables thread-safe parsing when paired with a reentrant Flex scanner (%option reentrant bison-bridge). The driver owns the scanner, symbol table, and result.
body: /* Inject driver reference into parser constructor and yylex */

Push Parser Skeleton.description: Bison push-mode parser using %define api.push-pull push. Instead of calling yyparse() which drives the scanner, you feed tokens one at a time via yypush_parse(). Ideal for event loops, async I/O, and coroutines. A usage skeleton is included as a comment at the bottom.
body: *   return status;  // 0 = success, 1 = syntax error, 2 = OOM
